accept: CLAUDE.md's canonical loop has an explicit review step (probably between work and close, or between close and push). The shape of 'review' is concrete: who is the reviewer (sub-agent type), what's the input, what's the threshold for halting vs landing.
Lessons from the first review (1-4 above) are captured in CLAUDE.md's versioning-rationale section so the second review prompt can reference them.

Pre-implementation review (review the plan before work) and post-implementation review (review the diff before close) are at minimum mentioned, with a default for which the loop runs and a deferral path for the other.
Trade-off captured: review every issue vs review every Nth vs review only changes touching certain paths. Pick a default with rationale.
payload.description:
Per Andrew 2026-05-10: the canonical work loop in CLAUDE.md should grow a review step. The 2026-05-10 codebase review (act-da03) returned 13 findings, 4 of which were >70%-confidence high-severity bugs. Without a review step, agents land work that introduces correctness gaps invisible to the test suite (see act-d79b 'act ready returns non-open' — passed all tests, broke the actual loop semantics).

Also capture lessons from the first review pass for the second one to be better:
1. Review-as-task in act gives the audit trail (act-da03 worked well).
2. Reviewer cited a stale commit hash at the top of its report (b928d4d, the initial scaffold) but reviewed actual current code — agent was given the cwd, not a specific ref, and confused its intro line with reality. Future reviewer prompts should pin the commit explicitly.
3. Confidence filter (>70%) eliminated taste-level nits and produced load-bearing findings only — keep this default.
4. Listing what's working well at the end was useful guidance for what NOT to break — preserve that section.
payload.title: CLAUDE.md should include a review step in the canonical loop
